description_long: This object is a long, double-edged sword with a straight, tapered blade that comes to a sharp point. The blade is metallic and appears to be forged from steel, exhibiting a dark, matte finish. The crossguard is straight and simple, extending horizontally to provide hand protection, and is also made of metal. The handle is cylindrical, slightly contoured for grip, and ends in a rounded pommel, which may serve as a counterweight. There are no visible engravings or decorative elements, giving the sword a utilitarian and functional appearance. The overall design suggests it is modeled after a classic European longsword, suitable for both cutting and thrusting.
description: A straight, double-edged steel sword with a simple crossguard and rounded pommel, featuring a dark, matte finish and no ornamentation.
description_view_0: The sword is seen edge-on, showing the thin profile of the blade and the rounded pommel.
description_view_1: The sword is viewed from the flat side, clearly displaying the double-edged blade, straight crossguard, and cylindrical handle.
description_view_2: Another edge-on view, mirroring view 0, highlighting the slimness of the blade and the symmetry of the handle and pommel.
description_view_3: A flat-side view similar to view 1, showing the full length of the blade, crossguard, and handle.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, straight, metal, rounded pommel, no visible grip texture
